title: Cuvette carrier
abstract: A cuvette carrier (100) comprising: a plurality of walls (101) defining a holding volume (102) for a cuvette (109); a first (103) and second (104) transmissive region included in the plurality of walls (101); and a first optical polariser (103) arranged to polarise light passing through the first transmissive region (103).
claims:
Claims 1. A cuvette carrier comprising: a plurality of walls defining a holding volume for a cuvette; a first and second transmissive region included in the plurality of walls; and a first optical polariser arranged to polarise light passing through the first transmissive region. 2. The cuvette carrier of claim 1 , further comprising a second optical polariser arranged to polarise light passing through the second transmissive region. 3. The cuvette carrier of claim 2, wherein an axis of polarisation of the first optical polariser is orthogonal to an axis of polarisation of the second optical polariser. 4. The cuvette carrier of claim 2, wherein an axis of polarisation of the first optical polariser is parallel to an axis of polarisation of the second optical polariser. 5. The cuvette carrier of any preceding claim, wherein the first transmissive region and second transmissive region are located in a first wall of the plurality of walls. 6. The cuvette carrier of any preceding claim, further comprising: a third transmissive region located in a second wall of the plurality of walls; and a third optical polariser arranged to polarise light passing through the third transmissive region. 7. The cuvette carrier of claim 6, wherein an axis of polarisation of the third optical polariser is orthogonal to an axis of polarisation of the first optical polariser. 8. The cuvette carrier of claim 6, wherein an axis of polarisation of the third optical polariser is parallel to an axis of polarisation of the first optical polariser. 9. The cuvette carrier of any of claims 6 to 8, further comprising a fourth transmissive region. 10. The cuvette carrier of claim 9, further comprising a fourth optical polariser arranged to polarise light passing through the fourth transmissive region. 1 1. The cuvette carrier of claims 9 or 10, wherein an axis of polarisation of the fourth optical polariser is parallel to an axis of polarisation of the second optical polariser. 12. The cuvette carrier of any of claims 9 to 1 1 , wherein the fourth transmissive region is located in the second wall. 13. The cuvette carrier of any of claims 6 to 12, wherein the first wall is adjacent to the second wall. 14. The cuvette carrier of any of claims 6 to 12, wherein the first wall is opposite the second wall. 15. The cuvette carrier of claim 13, as dependent upon any of claims 9 to 1 1 , wherein the fourth transmissive region is located in a third wall, wherein the third wall is opposite to the second wall. 16. The cuvette carrier of any of claims 9 to 1 1 , wherein each transmissive region is located in a different wall of the plurality of walls. 17. The cuvette carrier of claims 16, wherein the first transmissive region is opposite to the third transmissive region, and wherein the second transmissive region is opposite to the fourth transmissive region. 18. The cuvette carrier of any preceding claim, wherein at least one of the first, second, third, or fourth transmissive regions comprises an aperture in a wall. 19. The cuvette carrier of any preceding claim, wherein the first and second transmissive regions are adjacent to each other so as to form a continuous transmissive region. 20. The cuvette carrier of any of claims 9 to 14, wherein the third and fourth transmissive regions are adjacent to each other so as to form a continuous transmissive region. 21. The cuvette carrier of any proceeding claim, wherein the optical polariser or optical polarisers are configured to transmit light with a wavelength in the range 280- 2000nm. 22. The cuvette carrier of any preceding claim, wherein at least one of the first, second, third, or fourth optical polarisers is arranged to polarise all of the light passing through the first, second, third or fourth transmissive region respectively. 23. The cuvette carrier of any preceding claim, wherein the holding volume is adapted to receive a 10mm x 10mm sample cuvette . 24. The cuvette carrier of any preceding claim, further comprising an insert adapted to fit into the holding volume, the insert comprising a plurality of walls defining an inner volume that is smaller than the holding volume . 25. The cuvette carrier of claim 24, wherein a horizontal cross-section of the inner volume of the insert has dimensions of approximately 3mm x 3mm. 26. The cuvette carrier of any preceding claim, wherein an external horizontal cross-region of the cuvette carrier has dimensions of approximately 12.5mm x 12.5mm. 27. The cuvette carrier of any preceding claim, wherein the plurality of walls comprise a metal. 28. The cuvette carrier of claim 27, wherein the plurality of walls comprise aluminium or stainless steel. 29. The cuvette carrier of any preceding claim, wherein the plurality of walls are substantially covered in a protective coating. 30. The cuvette carrier of claim 29, wherein the plurality of walls comprise aluminium, and the protective coating is an anodised layer. 3 1. The cuvette carrier of any preceding claim, wherein at least one of the optical polarisers is configured to be removable by a user. 32. The cuvette carrier of any preceding claim, wherein the plurality of walls define a substantially cuboidal holding volume. 33. The cuvette carrier of any preceding claims, wherein the exterior shape of the cuvette carrier is substantially cuboidal. 34. The cuvette carrier of any preceding claim, further comprising a narrow band filter arranged to filter light passing through the first and/or third transmissive regions. 35. An apparatus for measuring particle size distribution of a sample by light scattering comprising: a light source for generating a beam of light; a cuvette carrier according to any of claims 1 to 34; a cuvette placed in the holding volume of the cuvette carrier; and a detector for detecting scattered light. 36. A method of characterising particles dispersed in a sample, the method comprising: placing the sample in a cuvette; placing the cuvette in a cuvette carrier, the cuvette carrier comprising: a plurality of walls defining a holding volume for a cuvette; a first and second transmissive region included in the plurality of walls; and 

 a first optical polariser arranged to polarise light passing through the first transmissive region; illuminating the sample with a light beam from a light source, the light beam passing through the second transmissive region of the cuvette carrier; and detecting scattered light from the sample produced by the interaction of the light beam with the particles, the scattered light passing through the first optical polariser. 37. The method of claim 36, wherein back-scattered, side-scattered, or forward- scattered light is detected. 38. The method of claims 36 or 37, wherein the method further comprises: replacing the first optical polariser with a replacement optical polariser, the replacement optical polariser having an axis of polarisation that is orthogonal to the axis of polarisation of the first optical polariser; and detecting light scattered from the sample that passes through the replacement optical polariser. 39. The method of claims 36 or 37, wherein the cuvette carrier further comprises a third transmissive region, a fourth transmissive region, and third optical polariser arranged to polarise light passing through the third transmissive region, the method further comprising; rotating the cuvette carrier such that the test beam passes through the fourth transmissive region of the cuvette carrier; and detecting light scattered from the sample that passes through the third optical polariser. 40. The method of any of claims 36 to 39, wherein the cuvette carrier is according to any of claims 1 to 34.
